name: orca-md-editor
description: A fast, clean WYSIWYG editor for .md — drag-and-drop reorder, project-wide search, and a rich toolbar, all while keeping your Markdown source pristine and diff-friendly.

commands.title: Open Orca MD Editor to the Side

orcaEditor.claudeAutoInsert.description: Copy @file button: insert @file directly into the Claude Code chat input instead of just copying it — requires briefly opening a text editor for the file then closing it right away (a mechanism required by Claude Code; you'll see a brief flicker). Default off: navigates to the chat tab, focuses the input, and copies @file to the clipboard so you can paste with ⌘V.

orcaEditor.autoOpenToc.description: Automatically open the Table of Contents panel whenever a Markdown file is opened in WYSIWYG Preview.

orcaEditor.showLineNumbers.description: Show line numbers (from the original Markdown file) in a gutter to the left of each content block (paragraph, heading, list, table, code, mermaid...), like the line-number gutter in raw text mode.

enumDescriptions: Only search .md/.markdown files in the workspace.
Search all text files in the workspace (respecting .gitignore and default excludes like node_modules, dist, images, binaries).
orcaEditor.crossFileSearch.scope.description: Default scope for the cross-file search triggered from a text selection in the preview. Can be overridden per-search via the dropdown in the results popover.

enumDescriptions: Save into an `assets/` subfolder next to the Markdown file being edited.
Save into the folder specified by orcaEditor.assetsPaste.customFolderPath.
orcaEditor.assetsPaste.location.description: Where to save images pasted from the clipboard or dropped into the editor, and files dropped from outside the editor. Renamed from orcaEditor.pasteImage.* (default folder changed from images/ to assets/) when drag-and-drop support was added — existing images already saved under images/ are not moved.

orcaEditor.assetsPaste.customFolderPath.description: Folder to save pasted/dropped images and dropped files into when orcaEditor.assetsPaste.location is "customFolder". Relative paths resolve against the workspace folder containing the Markdown file; absolute paths are used as-is.

orcaEditor.assetsPaste.orphanCleanup.ena.description: On save, permanently delete pasted/dropped images that are no longer referenced anywhere in this file (and not referenced by sibling Markdown files in the same folder). If the reference reappears later (e.g. via undo), the image is restored automatically. Does not apply to non-image files dropped into the editor — those are never auto-deleted.

orcaEditor.readability.enabled.description: Turn on Reading Mode: apply the reading preset (measure/typography) for a comfortable long-form reading experience. This is the starting default for newly opened tabs; the Reading Mode button on the toolbar applies only to the current tab for the session and is not saved back here.

enumDescriptions: Comfortable Reading: ~70ch measure, roomy line-height, serif reading font.
Default: current behavior — full width, follow VS Code theme/font.
Compact: ~90ch measure, tighter spacing to fit more on screen.
Dyslexia-friendly: accessibility font (Atkinson Hyperlegible / OpenDyslexic if installed) with increased letter/word/line spacing.
Academic Paper: narrow ~62ch column, classic book serif, roomy line-height — an essay/paper look (ai-2027.com style). Best paired with the Paper palette.
orcaEditor.readability.preset.description: Which reading preset to apply when Reading Mode is on — the starting default for newly opened tabs. Picking a preset from the caret dropdown on the toolbar Reading Mode button applies to the current tab only (not saved back here).

enumDescriptions: Follow the VS Code theme colors (current behavior).
Warm ivory background with near-black text (reduced glare/halation).
Deep grey background with ivory text (reduced halation).
Warm low-blue sepia — easy on the eyes at night.
Paper: bright warm-white (#fffff8) background with near-black text — a printed-page look (ai-2027.com style). Best paired with the Academic Paper preset.
orcaEditor.readability.palette.description: Reading color palette applied when Reading Mode is on — the starting default for newly opened tabs. Sepia and the hand-tuned Light/Dark palettes are designed for long-form reading (WCAG AA+ body contrast). Picking a palette from the toolbar applies to the current tab only (not saved back here).

orcaEditor.readability.fontFamily.description: Optional reading font override applied in Reading Mode. Type the name of a font installed on your machine (nothing is bundled/downloaded). Leave empty to use the preset's default font stack.

orcaEditor.readability.zen.description: Focus Mode: hide the toolbar and line-number gutter and center the text column. This is the starting default for newly opened tabs; the Focus button on the toolbar applies only to the current tab for the session (not saved back here). Press Esc to exit.

scripts.test: npm run test:roundtrip && npm run test:unit && npm run test:webview
